terms.definition: Protecting the country from enemies and dangers. India's defence is handled by the armed forces - the Army, Navy, and Air Force.
terms.exampleUsage: The defence forces protect our borders day and night.

terms.definition: The military organisations of a country that protect it. India has three armed forces: the Army (land), Navy (sea), and Air Force (sky).
terms.exampleUsage: People who join the armed forces are trained to serve and protect our country.

terms.definition: A person who holds a position of authority and leadership in the armed forces. Officers lead teams of soldiers, sailors, or airmen.
terms.exampleUsage: Wahida became a defence officer and led her team with courage.

terms.definition: The ability to do something brave even when you are scared. Soldiers and officers show courage every day to keep the country safe.
terms.exampleUsage: It takes great courage to serve in the armed forces and face dangers.

terms.definition: A fixed, unfair idea about a group of people. For example, thinking that only boys can be soldiers is a stereotype.
terms.exampleUsage: Women in the armed forces have broken the stereotype that only men can be brave.

terms.definition: Following rules and training yourself to behave in an orderly way. In the armed forces, discipline is very important for teamwork and safety.
terms.exampleUsage: Defence officers follow strict discipline, from waking up early to keeping their uniforms neat.

terms.definition: Love for one's country and the willingness to serve and protect it. People in the armed forces show patriotism by defending the nation.
terms.exampleUsage: Singing the national anthem fills us with patriotism and pride.

terms.definition: The line that separates one country from another. Indian soldiers guard the borders to keep the country safe.
terms.exampleUsage: Indian soldiers stand guard at the border even in freezing cold weather.

terms.definition: A national celebration on 26th January marking the day India's Constitution came into effect in 1950. A grand parade with the armed forces is held in New Delhi.
terms.exampleUsage: On Republic Day, we watch the parade of tanks, soldiers, and colourful floats on television.

terms.definition: A person whose behaviour and achievements inspire others to follow a similar path. Women officers in the armed forces are role models for young girls.
terms.exampleUsage: Wahida is a role model for girls who dream of joining the armed forces.
